dataAvailability.executive: Russia operates as a managed democracy where opposition parties exist but face systematic restrictions. Current opposition movements remain largely in exile or face criminal prosecution, with the Yabloko party experiencing intensified legal pressure ahead of the 2026 elections.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Systematic restrictions on opposition candidates and media access limit genuine competition
elections.electionWatchReason: No elections are imminent and leadership remains stable with Putin continuing his fifth term and Mishustin reconfirmed as Prime Minister in May 2024

lastElection.notes: United Russia retained majority control with 324 of 450 seats following multi-day voting.

nextElection.notes: Regular five-year parliamentary elections for all 450 seats scheduled for September 2026.

lastElection.notes: Vladimir Putin re-elected for fifth presidential term with over 87% of the vote.

nextElection.notes: Next presidential election scheduled for 2030 under current six-year term.

dataAvailability.executive: Modi sworn in for third consecutive term June 9, 2024, becoming second PM after Nehru to serve three consecutive terms. For first time since 2014, BJP lacks outright majority and governs through coalition with key partners Telugu Desam Party (16 seats) and Janata Dal United (12 seats). NDA controls both houses of parliament with 293 of 543 Lok Sabha seats.

elections.electionWatchReason: General election completed June 2024 with clear winner confirmed and third Modi government formed June 9, 2024

lastElection.notes: Seven-phase election concluded June 1, 2024. BJP-led NDA won 293 seats, BJP alone won 240 seats (down from 303 in 2019). Results declared June 4-5, 2024.

nextElection.notes: Constitutional maximum five-year term expires June 2029.

lastElection.notes: Droupadi Murmu won with 676,803 electoral votes (64.03%), defeating Yashwant Sinha. Took oath July 25, 2022.

nextElection.notes: Five-year presidential term expires July 2027.

dataAvailability.executive: PML-N leads coalition government with PPP, MQM-P, and other smaller parties controlling over two-thirds majority in National Assembly. PTI-backed independents form largest opposition bloc but many joined Sunni Ittehad Council for reserved seats allocation. Pakistan's military establishment continues to wield significant behind-the-scenes influence over political process.

elections.electionWatchReason: Government fully formed and stable since March 2024 coalition agreement

lastElection.notes: PTI-backed independents won plurality (93 seats) but PML-N formed coalition government with PPP and smaller parties.

nextElection.notes: Scheduled by constitutional five-year term limit.

lastElection.notes: Asif Ali Zardari elected president by electoral college comprising National Assembly, Senate and provincial assemblies.

nextElection.notes: Five-year presidential term concurrent with National Assembly.

dataAvailability.executive: General Secretary Xi Jinping holds supreme power as leader of the Chinese Communist Party, though formally serves as President. The political system is a one-party state where the CCP exercises control over all government institutions including the National People's Congress.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: One-party state. The Chinese Communist Party holds a monopoly on political power. National People's Congress delegate selection is controlled by the party.
elections.ipu_not_applicable_reason: One-party state where electoral processes are non-competitive and controlled by the Chinese Communist Party
elections.electionWatchReason: No upcoming competitive elections or power transitions expected in one-party system

lastElection.notes: 14th National People's Congress delegates selected through party-controlled process from October 2022 to February 2023. First session held March 5-13, 2023.

nextElection.notes: Expected party-controlled delegate selection process for 15th NPC session.

lastElection.notes: Xi Jinping unanimously confirmed for third term as President by NPC. Li Qiang confirmed as Premier.

nextElection.notes: Expected NPC confirmation process for next presidential and premier terms.

dataAvailability.executive: Starmer faces significant political challenges including poor polling numbers, internal party discontent, and leadership speculation from within Labour ranks. Recent by-election losses to Green Party indicate declining government support. Multiple reports suggest potential leadership challenge discussions among Labour MPs, though Starmer remains defiant about continuing as PM.

elections.electionWatchReason: No national elections scheduled within 3 days and last general election (July 2024) concluded with clear winner

lastElection.notes: Labour won 411 seats with a landslide majority, ending 14 years of Conservative rule. Keir Starmer became Prime Minister.

nextElection.notes: Due by August 2029 under the Dissolution and Calling of Parliament Act 2022. Parliament automatically dissolves 9 July 2029 unless dissolved earlier.

lastElection.notes: Prime Minister appointed after Labour won parliamentary majority. Keir Starmer appointed by King Charles III on 5 July 2024.

nextElection.notes: Next UK general election due by August 2029 determines new government composition.

nextElection.notes: Regular election before this date as required by Basic Law.

dataAvailability.executive: The UAE operates as a federal constitutional monarchy where the president is elected by the Federal Supreme Council (comprising the seven emirate rulers) every five years, and the prime minister is appointed by the president. Both positions are effectively hereditary within the Al Nahyan and Al Maktoum ruling families respectively. The Federal National Council serves only in an advisory capacity with limited powers.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Federal constitutional monarchy where executive positions are hereditary. The Federal National Council is half-appointed, half indirectly elected via a limited electorate of ~400,000 selected by emirate rulers. Political parties are banned.

lastElection.notes: Only half of the 40-seat FNC is indirectly elected by an appointed electorate of ~399,000. The body is advisory only. Results were certified on October 13, 2023.

nextElection.notes: Expected based on four-year terms. Exact date typically announced closer to election year.

dataAvailability.executive: King Salman remains the formal head of state but Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has consolidated de facto power as Prime Minister since 2022. Municipal elections have been postponed indefinitely under Vision 2030. The Consultative Assembly consists of 150 appointed members with advisory functions only.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Absolute monarchy with no national elections for executive or legislative positions. The Consultative Assembly is fully appointed by royal decree.
elections.ipu_not_applicable_reason: One-party state with no competitive national elections. The Shura Council is entirely appointed by the King.

dataAvailability.executive: Current coalition includes Likud, Religious Zionist Party, Otzma Yehudit, Shas, and New Hope. United Torah Judaism and parts of other coalition parties have withdrawn or reduced participation over conscription issues and other policy disputes. Netanyahu's government faces significant challenges including Iran war, Gaza aftermath, judicial reform controversy, and ultra-Orthodox conscription crisis. Multiple opposition parties have formed for 2026 elections including Bennett 2026 and Yashar parties.

elections.electionWatchReason: Election scheduled for October 27, 2026 with stable government continuing to function normally

lastElection.notes: Netanyahu's Likud-led bloc won 64/120 seats, returning him to power after coalition negotiations.

nextElection.notes: Legal deadline by Supreme Court ruling. Government may call snap elections earlier due to coalition pressures and budget concerns.

nextElection.notes: Prime Minister emerges from parliamentary coalition formation after elections.

dataAvailability.executive: Lula announced in October 2025 that he will seek a fourth term in the 2026 election. Jair Bolsonaro has been convicted and sentenced to 27 years in prison for attempting a coup, making him ineligible to run. His son Flávio Bolsonaro has emerged as a leading opposition candidate. Brazilian democracy remains competitive with free and fair elections despite political polarization.

Nigeria operates on a four-year electoral cycle with the next elections scheduled for February 2027.

dataAvailability.executive: Power is heavily concentrated in President Ilham Aliyev and his extended family since 2003. International organizations including Freedom House, IDEA, and OSCE consistently rate Azerbaijan as authoritarian with unfree elections. Opposition parties face systematic repression and have boycotted recent elections. New Azerbaijan Party maintains control through state resources, electoral commissions, and restrictions on political opposition.

dataAvailability.executive: Somalia faces a severe constitutional crisis as President Mohamud's term expires May 15, 2026 under the 2012 Provisional Constitution, but March 2026 amendments extending the mandate to five years are rejected by major opposition groups, Puntland, Jubaland, and Southwest states. Three federal member states have effectively withdrawn from the federal system amid disputes over the amendments, which critics view as consolidating executive power. Risk of parallel government formation if no agreement reached by constitutional deadline.

elections.electionWatchReason: Deep constitutional crisis with competing constitutional frameworks and mandate dates. President's term officially expires May 15, 2026 under 2012 constitution, but March 2026 constitutional amendments extending mandate to 5 years are rejected by major opposition groups and federal states.

lastElection.notes: Parliamentary elections concluded March 2022 through indirect clan-based voting with 255 of 275 House seats and 54 Senate seats filled.

nextElection.notes: Constitutional amendments passed March 2026 extended legislative mandate to five years, moving elections to 2027. Opposition rejects this timeline and demands elections by May 2026.

lastElection.notes: Hassan Sheikh Mohamud defeated incumbent Mohamed Abdullahi Mohamed in third round with over 165 votes from parliament.

nextElection.notes: March 2026 constitutional amendments extended presidential term to five years. Opposition demands election by May 15, 2026 under original four-year mandate.

dataAvailability.executive: Egypt operates as an authoritarian system under President Sisi with systematic repression of genuine opposition. The 2025 parliamentary elections were marred by widespread violations, candidate disqualifications, and low genuine competition, leading to multiple annulments and reruns. Recent protests in 2024 led to mass arrests. Current regional tensions with Iran war affecting energy prices and economic policy. Parliament serves largely as rubber-stamp institution.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Authoritarian system with systematic repression of opposition, limited candidate access, media control, and widespread electoral violations
elections.electionWatchReason: Parliamentary elections concluded January 2026 with results finalized, new parliament formed

lastElection.notes: House of Representatives elections held in phases with multiple runoffs due to widespread violations. Nation's Future Party won approximately 227 seats, Homeland Defenders Party 87 seats. Elections marked by low genuine competition and numerous annulments.

nextElection.notes: Sisi's third six-year term runs until 2030. Constitutional amendments allow potential further term extensions.

dataAvailability.executive: Colombia faces significant security challenges with over 60 political leaders killed during the 2026 campaign period. Congressional results show continued fragmentation with no party achieving majority control in either chamber.

lastElection.notes: Pacto Histórico won plurality in Senate with 25 seats. Democratic Center took most House seats with 32. Results fragmented across both chambers with no party achieving majority. Over 60 political leaders killed during campaign.

nextElection.notes: Next congressional elections scheduled for March 2030.

lastElection.notes: Gustavo Petro defeated Rodolfo Hernández in runoff with 50.4% of votes. Turnout 58.17%, highest since 1998. Petro became first leftist president in Colombian history.

nextElection.notes: First round scheduled for May 31, 2026. Leading candidates include Iván Cepeda (Pacto Histórico), Paloma Valencia (Democratic Center), and Abelardo de la Espriella (far-right). Petro constitutionally barred from reelection.
nextElection.runoffCondition: if no candidate wins >50% of valid votes in round 1

dataAvailability.executive: Mulino's government coalition controls National Assembly through alliance with multiple parties totaling 44 of 71 seats, with Dana Castañeda as Assembly President. Strong relationship with former President Martinelli despite his legal issues. Mulino has maintained 78% approval rating after 100 days in office as of October 2024.

elections.electionWatchReason: Election concluded with clear winner confirmed and government transferred power on July 1, 2024

lastElection.notes: Fragmented result with no single-party majority. Independents won 20 seats, making them the largest single bloc.

nextElection.notes: Based on 5-year term cycle.

lastElection.notes: Mulino won with 34.2% in single-round election. Originally Martinelli's running mate, became candidate after Martinelli's disqualification.

nextElection.notes: Panama prohibits consecutive presidential terms; elections held every 5 years.

dataAvailability.executive: El Salvador has undergone severe democratic backsliding under Bukele with institutional capture of judiciary and legislature, ongoing state of emergency since March 2022 suspending constitutional rights, mass arbitrary arrests of over 80,000 people, and elimination of effective political competition. Constitutional reforms in July 2025 abolished presidential term limits and eliminated runoff elections. Freedom House rates the country as Partly Free with score declining from 56 to 47 out of 100 between 2023-2025. Multiple international organizations classify El Salvador as having transitioned to authoritarianism or hybrid regime status.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Authoritarian consolidation under Bukele has eliminated effective political competition through institutional capture, constitutional manipulation, mass arbitrary arrests, harassment of opposition, and uneven playing field
elections.electionWatchReason: Next elections scheduled for February 2027 with clear electoral processes in place

lastElection.notes: New Ideas won 54 of 60 seats (90% supermajority). Concurrent with presidential election. Electoral reforms favored ruling party through D'Hondt system adoption.

nextElection.notes: Scheduled concurrently with presidential and municipal elections. Term length unchanged at 3 years.

lastElection.notes: Bukele won 84.65% in landslide victory despite constitutional prohibition on consecutive terms. Supreme Court ruling enabled his candidacy.

nextElection.notes: Constitutional reforms approved July 2025 eliminated term limits, extended terms to 6 years, and moved election from 2029 to 2027 to synchronize with legislative elections.
nextElection.runoffCondition: Second round eliminated by constitutional reform

dataAvailability.executive: Ukraine remains under martial law since February 2022, extended until May 4, 2026. Constitution allows extension of presidential and parliamentary terms during martial law. All major parties agreed in 2023 to postpone elections until after war ends. Peace negotiations are ongoing but have not yielded breakthrough. Zelensky indicates readiness for elections if security guaranteed by allies within 60-90 days, but this remains contingent on ceasefire agreement. Prime Minister Svyrydenko appointed July 2025, replacing Denys Shmyhal as part of government reshuffle focused on wartime economic management and weapons production.

elections.suspensionReason: Elections suspended under martial law due to Russian invasion; all parties agreed to postpone until war ends
elections.electionWatchReason: No elections scheduled; martial law extended until May 4, 2026; peace negotiations ongoing but no election timeline established

lastElection.notes: Servant of the People won supermajority (254/450 seats). Elections suspended under martial law since February 2022.
Zelensky defeated Poroshenko 73% to 25%. Term extended under martial law provisions; parliamentary resolution affirms elections only after war ends.

electionDataModel.description: Each country's elections block contains competitiveElections (bool), nonCompetitiveReason (str|null), electionWatchActive (bool — true when an election is within 3 days or results are still pending), and legislative/executive sub-blocks each with lastElection and nextElection. Election objects contain: date, type, notes, runoffDate, runoffCondition. partyProfiles contains orientation, ideology tags, and key platforms for each party currently or recently in power.
